title: Intensely Crispy Japanese Chicken Bowl
description: A tangy and crispy Japanese dish featuring jerk and tandoor flavors, served in an earthenware bowl, keto-friendly and romantic.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together almond flour, jerk seasoning, and tandoor spice blend.
Dip each piece of chicken into the eggs and then coat in the almond flour mixture.
Heat coconut oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and cook the chicken until browned on all sides.
Transfer the chicken to a baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes.
While the chicken is baking, heat sesame oil in a small skillet over medium heat and cook the scallions until fragrant.
To assemble the bowls, place a portion of the chicken into an earthenware bowl and top with scallions and a drizzle of sesame oil.
Garnish with additional scallions if desired.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
